content.full_text:
SUPERSEDED BY 1998
AMENDMENTS TO
REGULATION 18530
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
December 1, 1997
Emma Solden
City Clerk, City of Santa Cruz
809 Center Street, Room 9
Santa Cruz, California 95060
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-97-426a
Dear Ms. Solden:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions ofthe
Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1 You are the city clerk for the City of Santa Cruz and you are
requesting advice on behalf ofthe Santa Cruz City Council.
On August 7, 1997, you requested an advice letter from the Commission regarding the
use ofpublic funds for a candidate brochure. (Solden Advice Letter, No. A-97-426.) Presently,
you are requesting the Commission to render another advice letter on the same issue after we
review additional material. You have enclosed two letters from your city attorney that set forth
his opinion regarding the use ofpublic funds for a candidate brochure.
Upon reviewing the additional material, we affirm our advice provided in our previous
letter to you as it pertains to the Political Reform Act. In that letter, we advised the City of Santa
Cruz that it could prepare and distribute a candidate brochure that follows the guidelines of
Election Code section 13307 without violating sections 85300 and 89001. In addition, we
advised the city that it could not place restrictions on a candidate’s inclusion in the brochure that
would have the effect of favoring incumbents, such as requiring a candidate to raise a specific
dollar amount and/or requiring a candidate to collect a specific number of qualified signatures
before including the candidate in the brochure. Finally, we advised the city that it could require
candidates to accept voluntary spending limits before including them in the brochure as long as
the brochure explicitly stated that only those candidates who accepted the voluntary spending
limit were included in the brochure.
Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-97-426
Page 2
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Julia Butcher
Graduate Legal Assistant, Legal Division
SGC:JB:jlw

sections.question_synthetic: May a city use public funds to prepare and distribute a candidate brochure under the Political Reform Act, and what restrictions may it place on candidate inclusion?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC affirmed its previous advice that a city may prepare and distribute a candidate brochure following Election Code section 13307 guidelines without violating the Political Reform Act, may not impose restrictions favoring incumbents (such as fundraising minimums or signature requirements), but may require candidates to accept voluntary spending limits if the brochure discloses this condition.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a follow-up/supplemental advice letter (marked as superseded by 1998 amendments). No separate QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers; instead advice is provided in narrative paragraphs. Previous advice letter reference (A-97-426) mentioned but full text not provided. Graduate Legal Assistant signed the letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May a city use public funds to prepare and distribute a candidate brochure under the Political Reform Act, and what restrictions may it place on candidate inclusion?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC affirmed its previous advice that a city may prepare and distribute a candidate brochure following Election Code section 13307 guidelines without violating the Political Reform Act, may not impose restrictions favoring incumbents (such as fundraising minimums or signature requirements), but may require candidates to accept voluntary spending limits if the brochure discloses this condition.

In that letter, we advised the City of Santa Cruz that it could prepare and distribute a candidate brochure that follows the guidelines of Election Code section 13307 without violating sections 85300 and 89001. embedding.summary: This is an FPPC follow-up advice letter (97-426(a)) affirming the Commission's previous advice to the City of Santa Cruz regarding the use of public funds for a candidate brochure under the Political Reform Act, confirming permissible guidelines and restrictions.
